Notes: Enhanced Green Fluorescent Protein. Humanized A. victoria GFP with F64L/S65T mutations. Standard fluorescent marker for protein localization and expression.

Host: mCherry red fluorescent protein. DsRed-derived monomer. Web fetch recommended for codon-optimized DNA sequence.

Notes: Bright monomeric green fluorescent protein from Branchiostoma lanceolatum. Brighter than EGFP. Web fetch for sequence.
Bright cyan fluorescent protein. Optimized for FRET donor applications. Web fetch for sequence.
Yellow fluorescent protein variant. Good for FRET acceptor with CFP. Web fetch for sequence.
Photinus pyralis luciferase. Chemiluminescent reporter. Requires D-luciferin substrate. Web fetch for sequence.
Renilla reniformis luciferase. Used with coelenterazine. Often paired with firefly luc for dual reporter assays. Web fetch for sequence.
Beta-galactosidase from E. coli lacZ. Cleaves X-gal (blue-white) or CPRG (colorimetric). Web fetch for sequence.
Small engineered luciferase (19 kDa). Very bright. Uses furimazine substrate. Promega-developed. Web fetch for sequence.
Bacterial phytochrome-derived near-infrared fluorescent protein. Requires biliverdin cofactor. Web fetch for sequence.
Wild-type Aequorea victoria GFP. Excitation 395nm (major) and 475nm (minor). sfGFP alias. Slightly less bright than EGFP but well-characterized original sequence.
